intentional_test_features: Red herrings: severe cramps, explosive diarrhoea and presyncope mimic a surgical abdomen, infectious enterocolitis and obstruction.
Important-not-to-miss: surgical abdomen, infective enterocolitis and significant dehydration must be excluded; the patient cannot keep fluids down.
Anchoring: 'known post-infectious IBS/mast cell flare' should not close off surgical/infective causes or under-weight dehydration.
Commission trap: IM antispasmodic/antiemetic and de-novo amitriptyline given in person do not translate to remote injections; presyncope needs assessment/IV fluids.
Telehealth limits: abdominal examination, BP and IV rehydration cannot be done over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
